description_long: This object is a dark, single-edged knife with a pronounced curve along its blade, reminiscent of a traditional kukri or similar utility blade. The blade appears robust and tapers to a sharp point, with a slightly irregular, hand-forged texture visible along its length. The handle is integrated into the same material as the blade, with a subtle widening at the end, possibly to improve grip and prevent slippage. There is no visible guard or crossguard separating the blade from the handle, giving it a streamlined, utilitarian appearance. The entire object is a uniform dark color, suggesting it is made from a single piece of metal, likely steel or iron. No decorative elements or ornamentation are present, emphasizing function over form.
description: A dark, curved, single-edged knife with a pointed tip and a simple, integrated handle. The surface is rough and utilitarian, with no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: The knife is shown from a side angle, highlighting its curved blade and the subtle widening at the handle's end.
description_view_1: A top-down view reveals the blade's narrow profile and the alignment of the handle with the blade.
description_view_2: The opposite side view displays the same pronounced curve and the continuous, unadorned surface of the knife.
description_view_3: A bottom-up view shows the thickness of the blade and handle, emphasizing the solid, monolithic construction.
attribute_handle_design: integrated, slightly widened end, rough metal texture
